title: Agent swarms and the new model economics
excerpt: We compared old and new agent swarms building SQLite from scratch and found that better coordination delivers similar quality at a fraction of the cost.
content_html:
Earlier this year, we ran experiments to test the limits of scaling agents to cooperate toward a goal. Our hypothesis was that this would unlock a new tier of task scale and complexity.

The flagship project was a long-running swarm building a web browser from scratch. It succeeded as a proof of concept, but fell far short of polished software.

That work was deliberately empirical. We started from a blank canvas and hill-climbed toward a stable, effective system. Since then, our goal has been to understand the agent swarm well enough to engineer it deliberately.

To test that progress, we returned to a task the old swarm had struggled with: building SQLite from scratch, in Rust, from nothing but its documentation.

Our initial results have been promising. We ran the old and new swarms on the same task, with the same models and the same time budget, and measured how much of a held-out SQL test suite each could pass.

The new swarm did better in every model configuration. Using Grok 4.5, it reached 80% in four hours, while the old swarm spiraled and had to be paused before its second hour.

We also varied which models did which jobs. In some runs, one model handled everything while in others, a frontier model planned while a fast, inexpensive model carried out the work. Every mix produced similar quality, but the costs varied enormously.1

Trees and leaves

Descriptions of large tasks naturally take the shape of trees, with a goal at the root that subdivides recursively into basic units of work. Our swarm has two roles, both organized around that same tree-like decomposition:

  • Planner agents, powered by the smartest models, split a goal into pieces and delegate them.
  • Worker agents, generally powered by faster and less expensive models, execute those pieces.

The design is a superset of more rigid orchestration systems. Rather than imposing a fixed topology on the problem, the swarm’s shape grows to cover the problem’s contours, and compute and context scale in proportion to the task’s complexity.

We think this is why the design generalizes to tasks as diverse as building a browser, solving math problems, and optimizing GPU kernels. We’ve also used it internally to find and fix vulnerabilities in open-source software, raise test coverage on our own codebase, and generate billions of tokens of synthetic training data.

What the tree does for memory

When a single agent takes on a complete task, it has to walk the entire tree itself, descending to each leaf while holding its ancestors, its current position, and the wider goal in context the whole time.

We think this explains why long-running single agents drift. They can either focus on the work in front of them and lose sight of the bigger picture, or hold the big picture and do a worse job on the piece.

In a swarm, a planner never implements, so its context never fills with low-level detail, and a worker never plans, so it can spend all its context on one narrow piece of work.

We suspect the ability to scale the agent swarm comes from this context efficiency, more than from parallelism itself. That efficiency is present in the swarm at every scale, which is why this decomposition helps agent performance even on moderately sized tasks.

There are echoes of this structure elsewhere. The economist Ronald Coase, asking why firms exist at all, argued that coordination costs grow faster than the work itself, so organizations settle into tiers of bounded units rather than letting everyone talk to everyone.

A version control system for agents

In an earlier post about the swarm, we noted that tools like Git and Cargo rely on coarse locks for concurrency control. This is fine for one developer but unworkable for the volume of work produced by hundreds of concurrent agents.

The browser swarm from earlier this year peaked at roughly 1,000 commits per hour on Git. The new system peaks at around 1,000 commits per second.

To facilitate this rate of activity, we built a new version control system (VCS) from scratch. Throughput was not the only reason to own this layer. Every change in the system passes through the VCS, so it is where collisions first become visible, and several of the coordination mechanisms in the next section are implemented directly inside of it.

Failure modes at 1,000 commits per second

Human engineering teams have standard coordination mechanisms like code review, ownership, standups, and merge queues. Those systems work at human tempo, but at the commit-rate of the swarm, we see failure modes that human teams don’t routinely encounter.

Split-brain design

Two planners, unaware of each other, implement the same concept in different ways in different parts of the codebase.

We fixed this through prompting. Planners make design decisions themselves rather than delegating them, and we require them to ensure that no two delegated subtrees decide the same question.

Contention between planners

A harder form of contention is when two planners know about each other and fight through back-and-forth changes over the same files.

The problem is two pictures of reality, and merge tooling can't fix a disagreement. Instead, we have agents record decisions in shared design docs. Code that depends on a decision carries a compile-checked reference back to its doc. When planners unknowingly contradict each other, a reconciler merges the docs and the references propagate the resolution downstream.

Merge conflicts

Within the swarm, agents constantly collide on the same files. In order to resolve a collision they would have to stop, absorb the other agent's context, and merge around it. Worker agents are bad at this and, in practice, either overwrite the other change or abandon their own.

To fix this, we created a system where a neutral third-party agent intervenes on merge conflicts and resolves them on behalf of all parties. Its only goal is to be impartial and efficient, similar to the way merge queues work in engineering teams.

Megafiles

Some files are particularly popular places for agents to work. Each agent might add only a small amount of code, and no single agent is responsible for keeping the files small.

These “megafiles” choke everything. They’re expensive to transport, diff, and merge, and become the site of constant collisions.

To fix this, we gave worker agents a way to flag bloated files. Once flagged, we block new commits and an outside agent decomposes the overgrown file into smaller modules.

Ossification

Agents have learned, from working in existing codebases with humans in the loop, not to touch core code even when it needs to change.

To fix this, we license intentional breakage. An agent that judges a core change worthwhile can make a focused patch outside its scope and leave a comment explaining why it did it.

The compiler carries the change through the rest of the system, and everything depending on the old design fails to build. Each agent that hits one of those errors finds the comment, reads the reasoning, and updates its own piece of work to match.

Review lenses

In a system that is both long-running and multi-agent, errors accumulate, and the swarm needs a way to correct itself before small mistakes become foundational.

We experimented with many kinds of review lenses, such as giving a review agent the worker's full transcript, or only its output, or nothing but the codebase. We also tried reviewers running on different models, with different training and a different personality.

No single lens catches everything, but decorrelated lenses stack, the way self-driving systems reach above-human reliability without any single perfect component. The compute spent on review is high return, since review is much cheaper than the work it audits. We suspect this stacked review system was a major contributor to the sustained quality of the runs.

Letting agents shape the environment

Stigmergy is the mechanism by which swarm organisms like ants and termites coordinate without direct communication. They shape the environment, and the environment shapes the next organism.

We had encoded rules like “keep notes” and “document decisions” in earlier runs because they seemed obviously good. In retrospect, they were letting agents institutionalize knowledge for their future selves and teammates.

We pushed this further with an experiment in self-authored, shared context we call the Field Guide. It’s a folder owned entirely by the agents, whose index.md is automatically injected into every agent at start. It is the agents’ job to curate what goes into the guide and their only constraint is a line budget.

The underlying logic of the guide is that model weights are frozen, so it’s precisely surprise encounters that are worth capturing so the next agent trajectory is shorter.

The Field Guide is an early experiment with promising results. We’d expect the benefits to be even larger on codebases agents don’t fully own. Training models to write for their successors, where better capture leads to better rewards, is an interesting follow-up area of research.

The SQLite experiment

We instructed the new version of the swarm, equipped with all the improvements described above, to implement the whole of the 835-page SQLite manual in Rust. We withheld the source code, test suites, SQLite binary, and internet access.

To measure progress, we graded against sqllogictest, a test suite from the SQLite project built to check that different database engines return the same results for the same queries. It contains millions of queries with known correct answers, and the grade is the fraction the swarm's database gets right. Progress shows up as a rising curve over the course of a run.

The swarm was never told the suite existed. After each run, we manually reviewed the code and the run itself, checking for cheating and shortcuts, and confirming the system was built out evenly, rather than just in the places where the tests look.

As you read the curves, keep in mind that agents chose their own strategies. Some built broad foundations and scored low for hours before a late spike while others went deep on one area, scored early, then plateaued while filling in the rest. Trends matter more than exact scores at exact moments.

Results across model mixes

We tested four configurations spanning capability and cost:

  1. GPT-5.5 as both planner and worker. A strong frontier model throughout.2
  2. Grok 4.5 as both planner and worker. Our cost-efficient frontier model, as a comparison point.
  3. Opus 4.8 as planner and Composer 2.5 as worker. Frontier judgment paired with efficient execution.
  4. Fable 5 as planner and Composer 2.5 as worker. To see whether a next-tier planner makes the hybrid more or less worthwhile.

The new harness outperformed the old in every mix.

The Fable 5 hybrid passed about two-thirds of the suite within the first hour. By the four-hour cutoff, the new runs sat between 73% and 85%, while the old runs ranged from 11% to 77%.

The old Grok 4.5 run was paused before its two-hour mark (more below). Every new configuration went on to pass 100% of the suite.

In the future we’d like to run the full N×N matrix of planner-worker combinations. For this cycle, the comparison that matters is between harness versions, and the behavioral differences turned out to be much larger than the score differences suggest.

A deep dive into the runs

Starting with the simplest measure of activity, we can see how the rate of commits varied for Grok 4.5 under the old harness versus the new. The old run produced 68,000 commits in its first two hours, roughly 70 times the new run's pace.

One reading is that it was more productive. Another is that most of those commits were busywork (thrash, contention, churn).

The merge conflict data points to the latter interpretation. The old run accumulated more than 70,000 conflicts before we paused it, accelerating rather than stabilizing, while the new run logged fewer than a thousand over its full four hours.

The conflicts concentrated where files grew largest. In the old run, the biggest files kept growing for the entire run and its single hottest file collected 7,771 conflicts, touched by 1,173 different agents. In the new run, the most contested file in the whole codebase saw 47.

The old swarm's biggest coordination failure — split-brain, or planners duplicating each other's work — showed up in the package structure. Rust code is organized into packages called crates, and in a project like this, each crate is roughly one major component.

The old run sprawled to 54 crates, including three separate SQL packages. The new run settled on nine crates early and never added another.

All of this shows up in the final codebase. In the Fable 5 mix, both the old and new swarms ultimately passed the full suite, but the old one needed 64,305 lines of engine code and the new one did it in 9,908. The Opus mix shows the same shape with 19,013 lines at a 97% grade under the old harness, and 4,645 lines at 100% under the new harness.

Model economics

We said at the top that every model mix produced similar quality while the costs varied enormously, from $1,339 for the Opus 4.8 hybrid to $10,565 for GPT-5.5 alone. The token data shows where that difference comes from.

The structure of the spend was consistent across every run, with workers carrying at least 69% of the tokens, and over 90% in most.

But the dollars split differently than the tokens, because planner tokens cost more. In the Opus 4.8 and Composer 2.5 mix, the Opus-as-planner produced a small fraction of the tokens but roughly two-thirds of the cost, while Composer-as-worker handled the vast majority of the tokens for the remaining third of the cost.

Few moments in a large task genuinely require frontier intelligence, such as the original decomposition, the design decisions, and certain trade-offs. Once a frontier planner has collapsed the ambiguity into a detailed, explicit instruction, less expensive models simply have to follow it. This is a huge potential source of cost savings. In the run that used GPT-5.5 for both planners and workers, the workers alone cost $9,373. In the run where Opus 4.8 did the planning and Composer 2.5 did the work, the entire worker fleet cost $411.

One detail worth noting comes from comparing the two hybrid runs. The Fable 5 planner ran up a slightly smaller bill than the Opus 4.8 planner, despite roughly twice the per-token price, because it used far fewer planning tokens. But the Fable run's workers went through several times as many tokens, and the run as a whole came out substantially more expensive.

Specs as prompts

Each jump in AI capability has raised the level of abstraction at which an engineer can work.

Autocomplete let engineers work one line of code at a time. Early models raised that to a block of code, and agents raised it to a file or a feature.

With swarms, the unit of work becomes the spec.

For that to work, the swarm has to actually follow the spec, which is what much of this post is about. We gave the swarm 835 pages of prose and it came back with a database. What was scarce in this experiment, and what we expect to be scarce in software engineering going forward, is the right description of intent.

Seen this way, the swarm starts to resemble a compiler. A compiler translates source code down to machine code through a series of intermediate steps. The swarm does something similar with intent. Planners parse a goal into task trees, then lower it step by step into executable work. The difference is that a compiler preserves meaning at every step while the swarm is probabilistic at every one. Everything described in this post exists to close that gap.

We invite you to explore the swarm's output. The codebase from the solo Opus 4.8 run is public at github.com/cursor/minisqlite. Based on our initial glance it looks great, but we have not done a deeper manual analysis. Take your own look, and tell us what you find.

  1. To get a sense of solo frontier costs, we also ran Opus 4.8 and Fable 5 on their own. We graded those runs only informally, so we draw no conclusions about their quality here, though from experience we would expect both models to do well. Their costs are shown in the chart as the hatched bars. ↩

  2. We had wanted GPT-5.6 Sol as the frontier configuration. The new model appears more sensitive to literal and emphasized wording than the others we tested, and we encountered runaway spirals unlike anything the other models produced. There wasn’t time to tune prompts for a model that arrived so recently, and tuning for one model while leaving the rest untouched would have made the comparison inaccurate, so we fell back to GPT-5.5. ↩

blocks.html_en: To test that progress, we returned to a task the old swarm had struggled with: building SQLite from scratch, in Rust, from nothing but its documentation.

sentences.en: To test that progress, we returned to a task the old swarm had struggled with: building SQLite from scratch, in Rust, from nothing but its documentation.
sentences.zh: 为了测试这一进展，我们回到了旧群体一直在努力完成的一项任务：用 Rust 从头开始​​构建 SQLite，除了它的文档之外什么都没有。

blocks.html_en: Our initial results have been promising. We ran the old and new swarms on the same task, with the same models and the same time budget, and measured how much of a held-out SQL test suite each could pass.

sentences.en: Our initial results have been promising.
sentences.zh: 我们的初步结果是有希望的。

sentences.en: We ran the old and new swarms on the same task, with the same models and the same time budget, and measured how much of a held-out SQL test suite each could pass.
sentences.zh: 我们使用相同的模型和相同的时间预算在同一任务上运行旧的和新的集群，并测量每个集群可以通过多少保留的 SQL 测试套件。

blocks.html_en: The new swarm did better in every model configuration. Using Grok 4.5, it reached 80% in four hours, while the old swarm spiraled and had to be paused before its second hour.

sentences.en: The new swarm did better in every model configuration.
sentences.zh: 新群体在每个模型配置中都表现更好。

sentences.en: Using Grok 4.5, it reached 80% in four hours, while the old swarm spiraled and had to be paused before its second hour.
sentences.zh: 使用 Grok 4.5，它在四个小时内达到了 80%，而旧的集群则呈螺旋式上升，必须在第二个小时之前暂停。

blocks.html_en: We also varied which models did which jobs. In some runs, one model handled everything while in others, a frontier model planned while a fast, inexpensive model carried out the work. Every mix produced similar quality, but the costs varied enormously.1

sentences.en: We also varied which models did which jobs.
sentences.zh: 我们还改变了哪些模型执行哪些工作。

sentences.en: In some runs, one model handled everything while in others, a frontier model planned while a fast, inexpensive model carried out the work.
sentences.zh: 在某些运行中，一个模型可以处理所有事情，而在其他运行中，则由前沿模型进行规划，而快速、廉价的模型则执行工作。

sentences.en: Every mix produced similar quality, but the costs varied enormously.1
sentences.zh: 每种混合物的质量相似，但成本差异巨大。1

blocks.html_en: Trees and leaves

sentences.en: Trees and leaves
sentences.zh: 树木和树叶

blocks.html_en: Descriptions of large tasks naturally take the shape of trees, with a goal at the root that subdivides recursively into basic units of work. Our swarm has two roles, both organized around that same tree-like decomposition:

sentences.en: Descriptions of large tasks naturally take the shape of trees, with a goal at the root that subdivides recursively into basic units of work.
sentences.zh: 对大型任务的描述自然采用树的形状，其根部的目标递归地细分为基本工作单元。

sentences.en: Our swarm has two roles, both organized around that same tree-like decomposition:
sentences.zh: 我们的群体有两个角色，都是围绕相同的树状分解组织的：

blocks.html_en: • Planner agents, powered by the smartest models, split a goal into pieces and delegate them.

sentences.en: Planner agents, powered by the smartest models, split a goal into pieces and delegate them.
sentences.zh: 规划代理在最智能的模型的支持下，将目标分解为多个部分并将其委托给他们。

blocks.html_en: • Worker agents, generally powered by faster and less expensive models, execute those pieces.

sentences.en: Worker agents, generally powered by faster and less expensive models, execute those pieces.
sentences.zh: 工作代理通常由更快、更便宜的模型驱动，执行这些部分。

blocks.html_en: The design is a superset of more rigid orchestration systems. Rather than imposing a fixed topology on the problem, the swarm’s shape grows to cover the problem’s contours, and compute and context scale in proportion to the task’s complexity.

sentences.en: The design is a superset of more rigid orchestration systems.
sentences.zh: 该设计是更严格的编排系统的超集。

sentences.en: Rather than imposing a fixed topology on the problem, the swarm’s shape grows to cover the problem’s contours, and compute and context scale in proportion to the task’s complexity.
sentences.zh: 群体的形状不是对问题强加固定的拓扑，而是不断增长以覆盖问题的轮廓，并根据任务的复杂性按比例进行计算和上下文缩放。

blocks.html_en: We think this is why the design generalizes to tasks as diverse as building a browser, solving math problems, and optimizing GPU kernels. We’ve also used it internally to find and fix vulnerabilities in open-source software, raise test coverage on our own codebase, and generate billions of tokens of synthetic training data.

sentences.en: We think this is why the design generalizes to tasks as diverse as building a browser, solving math problems, and optimizing GPU kernels.
sentences.zh: 我们认为这就是为什么该设计可以推广到构建浏览器、解决数学问题和优化 GPU 内核等多种任务。

sentences.en: We’ve also used it internally to find and fix vulnerabilities in open-source software, raise test coverage on our own codebase, and generate billions of tokens of synthetic training data.
sentences.zh: 我们还在内部使用它来查找和修复开源软件中的漏洞，提高我们自己的代码库的测试覆盖率，并生成数十亿个合成训练数据。

blocks.html_en: What the tree does for memory

sentences.en: What the tree does for memory
sentences.zh: 树对于记忆有什么作用

blocks.html_en: When a single agent takes on a complete task, it has to walk the entire tree itself, descending to each leaf while holding its ancestors, its current position, and the wider goal in context the whole time.

sentences.en: When a single agent takes on a complete task, it has to walk the entire tree itself, descending to each leaf while holding its ancestors, its current position, and the wider goal in context the whole time.
sentences.zh: 当单个智能体承担完整的任务时，它必须自己走遍整棵树，下降到每片叶子，同时始终保持其祖先、当前位置和更广泛的目标。

blocks.html_en: We think this explains why long-running single agents drift. They can either focus on the work in front of them and lose sight of the bigger picture, or hold the big picture and do a worse job on the piece.

sentences.en: We think this explains why long-running single agents drift.
sentences.zh: 我们认为这解释了为什么长期运行的单一代理会发生漂移。

sentences.en: They can either focus on the work in front of them and lose sight of the bigger picture, or hold the big picture and do a worse job on the piece.
sentences.zh: 他们要么专注于眼前的工作，而忽视了大局，要么只顾大局，却把工作做得更差。

blocks.html_en: In a swarm, a planner never implements, so its context never fills with low-level detail, and a worker never plans, so it can spend all its context on one narrow piece of work.

sentences.en: In a swarm, a planner never implements, so its context never fills with low-level detail, and a worker never plans, so it can spend all its context on one narrow piece of work.
sentences.zh: 在群体中，计划者从不实施，因此其上下文永远不会充满低级细节，而工作人员从不计划，因此它可以将所有上下文都花在一项狭窄的工作上。

blocks.html_en: We suspect the ability to scale the agent swarm comes from this context efficiency, more than from parallelism itself. That efficiency is present in the swarm at every scale, which is why this decomposition helps agent performance even on moderately sized tasks.

sentences.en: We suspect the ability to scale the agent swarm comes from this context efficiency, more than from parallelism itself.
sentences.zh: 我们怀疑扩展代理群的能力来自于这种上下文效率，而不是来自并行性本身。

sentences.en: That efficiency is present in the swarm at every scale, which is why this decomposition helps agent performance even on moderately sized tasks.
sentences.zh: 这种效率存在于各个规模的群体中，这就是为什么这种分解即使在中等规模的任务上也有助于代理的性能。

blocks.html_en: There are echoes of this structure elsewhere. The economist Ronald Coase, asking why firms exist at all, argued that coordination costs grow faster than the work itself, so organizations settle into tiers of bounded units rather than letting everyone talk to everyone.

sentences.en: There are echoes of this structure elsewhere.
sentences.zh: 这种结构在其他地方也有类似的体现。

sentences.en: The economist Ronald Coase, asking why firms exist at all, argued that coordination costs grow faster than the work itself, so organizations settle into tiers of bounded units rather than letting everyone talk to everyone.
sentences.zh: 经济学家罗纳德·科斯（Ronald Coase）在询问企业存在的原因时指出，协调成本的增长速度比工作本身的增长速度还要快，因此组织会进入有界单位的层级，而不是让每个人都与每个人交谈。

blocks.html_en: A version control system for agents

sentences.en: A version control system for agents
sentences.zh: 代理的版本控制系统

blocks.html_en: In an earlier post about the swarm, we noted that tools like Git and Cargo rely on coarse locks for concurrency control. This is fine for one developer but unworkable for the volume of work produced by hundreds of concurrent agents.

sentences.en: In an earlier post about the swarm, we noted that tools like Git and Cargo rely on coarse locks for concurrency control.
sentences.zh: 在之前关于 swarm 的文章中，我们注意到像 Git 和 Cargo 这样的工具依赖于粗略锁来进行并发控制。

sentences.en: This is fine for one developer but unworkable for the volume of work produced by hundreds of concurrent agents.
sentences.zh: 这对于一个开发人员来说很好，但对于数百个并发代理产生的工作量来说是不可行的。

blocks.html_en: The browser swarm from earlier this year peaked at roughly 1,000 commits per hour on Git. The new system peaks at around 1,000 commits per second.

sentences.en: The browser swarm from earlier this year peaked at roughly 1,000 commits per hour on Git.
sentences.zh: 今年早些时候，浏览器群在 Git 上的提交量达到了每小时约 1,000 次的峰值。

sentences.en: The new system peaks at around 1,000 commits per second.
sentences.zh: 新系统的峰值速度约为每秒 1,000 次提交。

blocks.html_en: To facilitate this rate of activity, we built a new version control system (VCS) from scratch. Throughput was not the only reason to own this layer. Every change in the system passes through the VCS, so it is where collisions first become visible, and several of the coordination mechanisms in the next section are implemented directly inside of it.

sentences.en: To facilitate this rate of activity, we built a new version control system (VCS) from scratch.
sentences.zh: 为了促进这种活动速度，我们从头开始构建了一个新的版本控制系统（VCS）。

sentences.en: Throughput was not the only reason to own this layer.
sentences.zh: 吞吐量并不是拥有该层的唯一原因。

sentences.en: Every change in the system passes through the VCS, so it is where collisions first become visible, and several of the coordination mechanisms in the next section are implemented directly inside of it.
sentences.zh: 系统中的每个更改都会通过 VCS，因此它是冲突首先变得可见的地方，并且下一节中的几个协调机制直接在其内部实现。

blocks.html_en: Failure modes at 1,000 commits per second

sentences.en: Failure modes at 1,000 commits per second
sentences.zh: 每秒 1,000 次提交的故障模式

blocks.html_en: Human engineering teams have standard coordination mechanisms like code review, ownership, standups, and merge queues. Those systems work at human tempo, but at the commit-rate of the swarm, we see failure modes that human teams don’t routinely encounter.

sentences.en: Human engineering teams have standard coordination mechanisms like code review, ownership, standups, and merge queues.
sentences.zh: 人类工程团队拥有标准的协调机制，例如代码审查、所有权、站立和合并队列。

sentences.en: Those systems work at human tempo, but at the commit-rate of the swarm, we see failure modes that human teams don’t routinely encounter.
sentences.zh: 这些系统按照人类的节奏工作，但按照群体的提交率，我们会看到人类团队通常不会遇到的故障模式。

blocks.html_en: Split-brain design

sentences.en: Split-brain design
sentences.zh: 裂脑设计

blocks.html_en: Two planners, unaware of each other, implement the same concept in different ways in different parts of the codebase.

sentences.en: Two planners, unaware of each other, implement the same concept in different ways in different parts of the codebase.
sentences.zh: 两个彼此不知情的规划者在代码库的不同部分以不同的方式实现相同的概念。

blocks.html_en: We fixed this through prompting. Planners make design decisions themselves rather than delegating them, and we require them to ensure that no two delegated subtrees decide the same question.

sentences.en: We fixed this through prompting.
sentences.zh: 我们通过提示解决了这个问题。

sentences.en: Planners make design decisions themselves rather than delegating them, and we require them to ensure that no two delegated subtrees decide the same question.
sentences.zh: 规划者自己做出设计决策，而不是委托他们，我们要求他们确保没有两个委托子树决定相同的问题。

blocks.html_en: Contention between planners

sentences.en: Contention between planners
sentences.zh: 规划者之间的争论

blocks.html_en: A harder form of contention is when two planners know about each other and fight through back-and-forth changes over the same files.

sentences.en: A harder form of contention is when two planners know about each other and fight through back-and-forth changes over the same files.
sentences.zh: 一种更困难的争用形式是，两个计划者彼此了解，并在同一文件的来回更改中发生争执。

blocks.html_en: The problem is two pictures of reality, and merge tooling can't fix a disagreement. Instead, we have agents record decisions in shared design docs. Code that depends on a decision carries a compile-checked reference back to its doc. When planners unknowingly contradict each other, a reconciler merges the docs and the references propagate the resolution downstream.

sentences.en: The problem is two pictures of reality, and merge tooling can't fix a disagreement.
sentences.zh: 问题是现实的两张图片，合并工具无法解决分歧。

sentences.en: Instead, we have agents record decisions in shared design docs.
sentences.zh: 相反，我们让代理在共享设计文档中记录决策。

sentences.en: Code that depends on a decision carries a compile-checked reference back to its doc.
sentences.zh: 取决于决策的代码将经过编译检查的引用返回到其文档。

sentences.en: When planners unknowingly contradict each other, a reconciler merges the docs and the references propagate the resolution downstream.
sentences.zh: 当规划者在不知不觉中相互矛盾时，协调器会合并文档，并且引用会将解决方案传播到下游。

blocks.html_en: Merge conflicts

sentences.en: Merge conflicts
sentences.zh: 合并冲突

blocks.html_en: Within the swarm, agents constantly collide on the same files. In order to resolve a collision they would have to stop, absorb the other agent's context, and merge around it. Worker agents are bad at this and, in practice, either overwrite the other change or abandon their own.

sentences.en: Within the swarm, agents constantly collide on the same files.
sentences.zh: 在群体中，代理不断地在相同的文件上发生冲突。

sentences.en: In order to resolve a collision they would have to stop, absorb the other agent's context, and merge around it.
sentences.zh: 为了解决碰撞，他们必须停下来，吸收其他智能体的上下文，并在其周围合并。

sentences.en: Worker agents are bad at this and, in practice, either overwrite the other change or abandon their own.
sentences.zh: 工作代理在这方面表现不佳，在实践中，要么覆盖其他更改，要么放弃自己的更改。

blocks.html_en: To fix this, we created a system where a neutral third-party agent intervenes on merge conflicts and resolves them on behalf of all parties. Its only goal is to be impartial and efficient, similar to the way merge queues work in engineering teams.

sentences.en: To fix this, we created a system where a neutral third-party agent intervenes on merge conflicts and resolves them on behalf of all parties.
sentences.zh: 为了解决这个问题，我们创建了一个系统，其中中立的第三方代理干预合并冲突并代表所有各方解决它们。

sentences.en: Its only goal is to be impartial and efficient, similar to the way merge queues work in engineering teams.
sentences.zh: 它的唯一目标是公正和高效，类似于工程团队中合并队列的工作方式。

blocks.html_en: Megafiles

sentences.en: Megafiles
sentences.zh: 巨型文件

blocks.html_en: Some files are particularly popular places for agents to work. Each agent might add only a small amount of code, and no single agent is responsible for keeping the files small.

sentences.en: Some files are particularly popular places for agents to work.
sentences.zh: 有些文件是特工特别受欢迎的工作场所。

sentences.en: Each agent might add only a small amount of code, and no single agent is responsible for keeping the files small.
sentences.zh: 每个代理可能只添加少量代码，并且没有任何一个代理负责保持文件较小。

blocks.html_en: These “megafiles” choke everything. They’re expensive to transport, diff, and merge, and become the site of constant collisions.

sentences.en: These “megafiles” choke everything.
sentences.zh: 这些“大文件”让一切都窒息。

sentences.en: They’re expensive to transport, diff, and merge, and become the site of constant collisions.
sentences.zh: 它们的运输、区分和合并成本高昂，并且成为不断发生碰撞的场所。

blocks.html_en: To fix this, we gave worker agents a way to flag bloated files. Once flagged, we block new commits and an outside agent decomposes the overgrown file into smaller modules.

sentences.en: To fix this, we gave worker agents a way to flag bloated files.
sentences.zh: 为了解决这个问题，我们为工作代理提供了一种标记膨胀文件的方法。

sentences.en: Once flagged, we block new commits and an outside agent decomposes the overgrown file into smaller modules.
sentences.zh: 一旦被标记，我们就会阻止新的提交，并且外部代理会将过度增长的文件分解为更小的模块。

blocks.html_en: Ossification

sentences.en: Ossification
sentences.zh: 骨化

blocks.html_en: Agents have learned, from working in existing codebases with humans in the loop, not to touch core code even when it needs to change.

sentences.en: Agents have learned, from working in existing codebases with humans in the loop, not to touch core code even when it needs to change.
sentences.zh: 代理通过在现有代码库中与人类参与循环的工作中了解到，即使需要更改，也不要触及核心代码。

blocks.html_en: To fix this, we license intentional breakage. An agent that judges a core change worthwhile can make a focused patch outside its scope and leave a comment explaining why it did it.

sentences.en: To fix this, we license intentional breakage.
sentences.zh: 为了解决这个问题，我们许可故意破坏。

sentences.en: An agent that judges a core change worthwhile can make a focused patch outside its scope and leave a comment explaining why it did it.
sentences.zh: 判断核心更改值得的代理可以在其范围之外制作一个重点补丁，并留下评论解释为什么这样做。

blocks.html_en: The compiler carries the change through the rest of the system, and everything depending on the old design fails to build. Each agent that hits one of those errors finds the comment, reads the reasoning, and updates its own piece of work to match.

sentences.en: The compiler carries the change through the rest of the system, and everything depending on the old design fails to build.
sentences.zh: 编译器将更改传递到系统的其余部分，并且依赖于旧设计的所有内容都无法构建。

sentences.en: Each agent that hits one of those errors finds the comment, reads the reasoning, and updates its own piece of work to match.
sentences.zh: 每个遇到这些错误之一的代理都会找到评论，阅读推理，并更新自己的工作以匹配。

blocks.html_en: Review lenses

sentences.en: Review lenses
sentences.zh: 回顾镜头

blocks.html_en: In a system that is both long-running and multi-agent, errors accumulate, and the swarm needs a way to correct itself before small mistakes become foundational.

sentences.en: In a system that is both long-running and multi-agent, errors accumulate, and the swarm needs a way to correct itself before small mistakes become foundational.
sentences.zh: 在一个长期运行的多智能体系统中，错误会累积，群体需要一种方法来纠正自己，以免小错误成为基础。

blocks.html_en: We experimented with many kinds of review lenses, such as giving a review agent the worker's full transcript, or only its output, or nothing but the codebase. We also tried reviewers running on different models, with different training and a different personality.

sentences.en: We experimented with many kinds of review lenses, such as giving a review agent the worker's full transcript, or only its output, or nothing but the codebase.
sentences.zh: 我们尝试了多种审​​查镜头，例如向审查代理提供工作人员的完整成绩单，或者仅提供其输出，或者只提供代码库。

sentences.en: We also tried reviewers running on different models, with different training and a different personality.
sentences.zh: 我们还尝试了在不同模型上运行、接受不同培训和不同个性的审稿人。

blocks.html_en: No single lens catches everything, but decorrelated lenses stack, the way self-driving systems reach above-human reliability without any single perfect component. The compute spent on review is high return, since review is much cheaper than the work it audits. We suspect this stacked review system was a major contributor to the sustained quality of the runs.

sentences.en: No single lens catches everything, but decorrelated lenses stack, the way self-driving systems reach above-human reliability without any single perfect component.
sentences.zh: 没有单一的镜头可以捕捉到一切，但是解相关的镜头堆叠，自动驾驶系统无需任何单一完美组件即可达到高于人类的可靠性。

sentences.en: The compute spent on review is high return, since review is much cheaper than the work it audits.
sentences.zh: 用于审核的计算量回报很高，因为审核比审核的工作便宜得多。

sentences.en: We suspect this stacked review system was a major contributor to the sustained quality of the runs.
sentences.zh: 我们怀疑这种堆积的审查系统是维持运行质量的主要因素。

blocks.html_en: Letting agents shape the environment

sentences.en: Letting agents shape the environment
sentences.zh: 让代理人塑造环境

blocks.html_en: Stigmergy is the mechanism by which swarm organisms like ants and termites coordinate without direct communication. They shape the environment, and the environment shapes the next organism.

sentences.en: Stigmergy is the mechanism by which swarm organisms like ants and termites coordinate without direct communication.
sentences.zh: Stigmergy是蚂蚁和白蚁等群体生物在没有直接交流的情况下进行协调的机制。

sentences.en: They shape the environment, and the environment shapes the next organism.
sentences.zh: 它们塑造环境，环境塑造下一个有机体。

blocks.html_en: We had encoded rules like “keep notes” and “document decisions” in earlier runs because they seemed obviously good. In retrospect, they were letting agents institutionalize knowledge for their future selves and teammates.

sentences.en: We had encoded rules like “keep notes” and “document decisions” in earlier runs because they seemed obviously good.
sentences.zh: 在早期的运行中，我们对“记笔记”和“记录决策”等规则进行了编码，因为它们看起来显然很好。

sentences.en: In retrospect, they were letting agents institutionalize knowledge for their future selves and teammates.
sentences.zh: 回想起来，他们让特工为未来的自己和队友将知识制度化。

blocks.html_en: We pushed this further with an experiment in self-authored, shared context we call the Field Guide. It’s a folder owned entirely by the agents, whose index.md is automatically injected into every agent at start. It is the agents’ job to curate what goes into the guide and their only constraint is a line budget.

sentences.en: We pushed this further with an experiment in self-authored, shared context we call the Field Guide.
sentences.zh: 我们在自己创作的共享环境中进行了一项实验，我们称之为“现场指南”，进一步推动了这一点。

sentences.en: It’s a folder owned entirely by the agents, whose index.md is automatically injected into every agent at start.
sentences.zh: 这是一个完全由代理拥有的文件夹，其 index.md 在启动时会自动注入到每个代理中。

sentences.en: It is the agents’ job to curate what goes into the guide and their only constraint is a line budget.
sentences.zh: 代理商的工作是策划指南中的内容，他们唯一的限制是直线预算。

blocks.html_en: The underlying logic of the guide is that model weights are frozen, so it’s precisely surprise encounters that are worth capturing so the next agent trajectory is shorter.

sentences.en: The underlying logic of the guide is that model weights are frozen, so it’s precisely surprise encounters that are worth capturing so the next agent trajectory is shorter.
sentences.zh: 该指南的基本逻辑是模型权重被冻结，因此正是值得捕获的意外遭遇，因此下一个智能体轨迹更短。

blocks.html_en: The Field Guide is an early experiment with promising results. We’d expect the benefits to be even larger on codebases agents don’t fully own. Training models to write for their successors, where better capture leads to better rewards, is an interesting follow-up area of research.

sentences.en: The Field Guide is an early experiment with promising results.
sentences.zh: 《现场指南》是一项早期实验，取得了可喜的成果。

sentences.en: We’d expect the benefits to be even larger on codebases agents don’t fully own.
sentences.zh: 我们预计代理不完全拥有的代码库的好处会更大。

sentences.en: Training models to write for their successors, where better capture leads to better rewards, is an interesting follow-up area of research.
sentences.zh: 训练模型为继任者写作，更好的捕捉会带来更好的回报，是一个有趣的后续研究领域。

blocks.html_en: The SQLite experiment

sentences.en: The SQLite experiment
sentences.zh: SQLite 实验

blocks.html_en: We instructed the new version of the swarm, equipped with all the improvements described above, to implement the whole of the 835-page SQLite manual in Rust. We withheld the source code, test suites, SQLite binary, and internet access.

sentences.en: We instructed the new version of the swarm, equipped with all the improvements described above, to implement the whole of the 835-page SQLite manual in Rust.
sentences.zh: 我们指示新版本的 swarm 配备了上述所有改进，以 Rust 实现整个 835 页的 SQLite 手册。

sentences.en: We withheld the source code, test suites, SQLite binary, and internet access.
sentences.zh: 我们保留了源代码、测试套件、SQLite 二进制文件和互联网访问权限。

blocks.html_en: To measure progress, we graded against sqllogictest, a test suite from the SQLite project built to check that different database engines return the same results for the same queries. It contains millions of queries with known correct answers, and the grade is the fraction the swarm's database gets right. Progress shows up as a rising curve over the course of a run.

sentences.en: To measure progress, we graded against sqllogictest, a test suite from the SQLite project built to check that different database engines return the same results for the same queries.
sentences.zh: 为了衡量进度，我们根据 sqllogictest 进行评分，sqllogictest 是 SQLite 项目的一个测试套件，旨在检查不同的数据库引擎对于相同的查询是否返回相同的结果。

sentences.en: It contains millions of queries with known correct answers, and the grade is the fraction the swarm's database gets right.
sentences.zh: 它包含数百万个已知正确答案的查询，等级是群体数据库正确的分数。

sentences.en: Progress shows up as a rising curve over the course of a run.
sentences.zh: 进度显示为跑步过程中的上升曲线。

blocks.html_en: The swarm was never told the suite existed. After each run, we manually reviewed the code and the run itself, checking for cheating and shortcuts, and confirming the system was built out evenly, rather than just in the places where the tests look.

sentences.en: The swarm was never told the suite existed.
sentences.zh: 蜂群从未被告知该套件的存在。

sentences.en: After each run, we manually reviewed the code and the run itself, checking for cheating and shortcuts, and confirming the system was built out evenly, rather than just in the places where the tests look.
sentences.zh: 每次运行后，我们手动检查代码和运行本身，检查作弊和快捷方式，并确认系统是均匀构建的，而不仅仅是在测试看起来的地方。

blocks.html_en: As you read the curves, keep in mind that agents chose their own strategies. Some built broad foundations and scored low for hours before a late spike while others went deep on one area, scored early, then plateaued while filling in the rest. Trends matter more than exact scores at exact moments.

sentences.en: As you read the curves, keep in mind that agents chose their own strategies.
sentences.zh: 当您阅读曲线时，请记住代理选择了自己的策略。

sentences.en: Some built broad foundations and scored low for hours before a late spike while others went deep on one area, scored early, then plateaued while filling in the rest.
sentences.zh: 有些人打下了广泛的基础，在几个小时内得分很低，然后才出现后期峰值，而另一些人则深入某一领域，早期得分，然后在填补其余部分的同时保持稳定。

sentences.en: Trends matter more than exact scores at exact moments.
sentences.zh: 趋势比确切时刻的确切分数更重要。

blocks.html_en: Results across model mixes

sentences.en: Results across model mixes
sentences.zh: 模型组合的结果

blocks.html_en: We tested four configurations spanning capability and cost:

sentences.en: We tested four configurations spanning capability and cost:
sentences.zh: 我们测试了四种涵盖功能和成本的配置：

blocks.html_en: • GPT-5.5 as both planner and worker. A strong frontier model throughout.2

sentences.en: GPT-5.5 as both planner and worker.
sentences.zh: GPT-5.5 既是规划者又是工作者。

sentences.en: A strong frontier model throughout.2
sentences.zh: 贯穿始终的强大前沿模型2

blocks.html_en: • Grok 4.5 as both planner and worker. Our cost-efficient frontier model, as a comparison point.

sentences.en: Grok 4.5 as both planner and worker.
sentences.zh: Grok 4.5 既是规划者又是工作者。

sentences.en: Our cost-efficient frontier model, as a comparison point.
sentences.zh: 我们的具有成本效益的前沿模型作为比较点。

blocks.html_en: • Opus 4.8 as planner and Composer 2.5 as worker. Frontier judgment paired with efficient execution.

sentences.en: Opus 4.8 as planner and Composer 2.5 as worker.
sentences.zh: Opus 4.8 作为计划者，Composer 2.5 作为工人。

sentences.en: Frontier judgment paired with efficient execution.
sentences.zh: 前沿判断力与高效执行力相结合。

blocks.html_en: • Fable 5 as planner and Composer 2.5 as worker. To see whether a next-tier planner makes the hybrid more or less worthwhile.

sentences.en: Fable 5 as planner and Composer 2.5 as worker.
sentences.zh: 《神鬼寓言 5》作为策划者，《Composer 2.5》作为工人。

sentences.en: To see whether a next-tier planner makes the hybrid more or less worthwhile.
sentences.zh: 看看下一级规划者是否会让混合变得更有价值还是更不值得。

blocks.html_en: The new harness outperformed the old in every mix.

sentences.en: The new harness outperformed the old in every mix.
sentences.zh: 新安全带在各个方面的表现都优于旧安全带。

blocks.html_en: The Fable 5 hybrid passed about two-thirds of the suite within the first hour. By the four-hour cutoff, the new runs sat between 73% and 85%, while the old runs ranged from 11% to 77%.

sentences.en: The Fable 5 hybrid passed about two-thirds of the suite within the first hour.
sentences.zh: 《神鬼寓言 5》混合动力车在第一个小时内就通过了大约三分之二的套房。

sentences.en: By the four-hour cutoff, the new runs sat between 73% and 85%, while the old runs ranged from 11% to 77%.
sentences.zh: 截至 4 小时截止时间，新运行的利用率在 73% 到 85% 之间，而旧运行的利用率在 11% 到 77% 之间。

blocks.html_en: The old Grok 4.5 run was paused before its two-hour mark (more below). Every new configuration went on to pass 100% of the suite.

sentences.en: The old Grok 4.5 run was paused before its two-hour mark (more below).
sentences.zh: 旧的 Grok 4.5 运行在两小时之前暂停（更多内容见下文）。

sentences.en: Every new configuration went on to pass 100% of the suite.
sentences.zh: 每个新配置都 100% 通过了套件。

blocks.html_en: In the future we’d like to run the full N×N matrix of planner-worker combinations. For this cycle, the comparison that matters is between harness versions, and the behavioral differences turned out to be much larger than the score differences suggest.

sentences.en: In the future we’d like to run the full N×N matrix of planner-worker combinations.
sentences.zh: 将来我们希望运行完整的 N×N 计划者-工作者组合矩阵。

sentences.en: For this cycle, the comparison that matters is between harness versions, and the behavioral differences turned out to be much larger than the score differences suggest.
sentences.zh: 对于这个周期，重要的是线束版本之间的比较，并且行为差异比分数差异显示的要大得多。

blocks.html_en: A deep dive into the runs

sentences.en: A deep dive into the runs
sentences.zh: 深入了解跑步

blocks.html_en: Starting with the simplest measure of activity, we can see how the rate of commits varied for Grok 4.5 under the old harness versus the new. The old run produced 68,000 commits in its first two hours, roughly 70 times the new run's pace.

sentences.en: Starting with the simplest measure of activity, we can see how the rate of commits varied for Grok 4.5 under the old harness versus the new.
sentences.zh: 从最简单的活动衡量标准开始，我们可以看到 Grok 4.5 在旧工具和新工具下的提交率有何变化。

sentences.en: The old run produced 68,000 commits in its first two hours, roughly 70 times the new run's pace.
sentences.zh: 旧运行在前两个小时内产生了 68,000 次提交，大约是新运行速度的 70 倍。

blocks.html_en: One reading is that it was more productive. Another is that most of those commits were busywork (thrash, contention, churn).

sentences.en: One reading is that it was more productive.
sentences.zh: 一种解读是它的生产力更高。

sentences.en: Another is that most of those commits were busywork (thrash, contention, churn).
sentences.zh: 另一个原因是，大多数提交都是忙碌的工作（冲击、争用、流失）。

blocks.html_en: The merge conflict data points to the latter interpretation. The old run accumulated more than 70,000 conflicts before we paused it, accelerating rather than stabilizing, while the new run logged fewer than a thousand over its full four hours.

sentences.en: The merge conflict data points to the latter interpretation.
sentences.zh: 合并冲突数据指向后一种解释。

sentences.en: The old run accumulated more than 70,000 conflicts before we paused it, accelerating rather than stabilizing, while the new run logged fewer than a thousand over its full four hours.
sentences.zh: 在我们暂停之前，旧运行累积了超过 70,000 个冲突，加速而不是稳定，而新运行在整个四个小时内记录了不到 1000 个冲突。

blocks.html_en: The conflicts concentrated where files grew largest. In the old run, the biggest files kept growing for the entire run and its single hottest file collected 7,771 conflicts, touched by 1,173 different agents. In the new run, the most contested file in the whole codebase saw 47.

sentences.en: The conflicts concentrated where files grew largest.
sentences.zh: 冲突集中在文件增长最大的地方。

sentences.en: In the old run, the biggest files kept growing for the entire run and its single hottest file collected 7,771 conflicts, touched by 1,173 different agents.
sentences.zh: 在旧的运行中，最大的文件在整个运行中不断增长，其单个最热门的文件收集了 7,771 个冲突，被 1,173 个不同的代理触及。

sentences.en: In the new run, the most contested file in the whole codebase saw 47.
sentences.zh: 在新的运行中，整个代码库中争议最多的文件为 47 个。

blocks.html_en: The old swarm's biggest coordination failure — split-brain, or planners duplicating each other's work — showed up in the package structure. Rust code is organized into packages called crates, and in a project like this, each crate is roughly one major component.

sentences.en: The old swarm's biggest coordination failure — split-brain, or planners duplicating each other's work — showed up in the package structure.
sentences.zh: 旧群体最大的协调失败——脑裂，或者规划者互相重复工作——出现在包结构中。

sentences.en: Rust code is organized into packages called crates, and in a project like this, each crate is roughly one major component.
sentences.zh: Rust 代码被组织成称为 crate 的包，在这样的项目中，每个 crate 大致都是一个主要组件。

blocks.html_en: The old run sprawled to 54 crates, including three separate SQL packages. The new run settled on nine crates early and never added another.

sentences.en: The old run sprawled to 54 crates, including three separate SQL packages.
sentences.zh: 旧版本有 54 个包，其中包括三个独立的 SQL 包。

sentences.en: The new run settled on nine crates early and never added another.
sentences.zh: 新的一批很早就确定了九个板条箱，并且再也没有添加过另一个。

blocks.html_en: All of this shows up in the final codebase. In the Fable 5 mix, both the old and new swarms ultimately passed the full suite, but the old one needed 64,305 lines of engine code and the new one did it in 9,908. The Opus mix shows the same shape with 19,013 lines at a 97% grade under the old harness, and 4,645 lines at 100% under the new harness.

sentences.en: All of this shows up in the final codebase.
sentences.zh: 所有这些都显示在最终的代码库中。

sentences.en: In the Fable 5 mix, both the old and new swarms ultimately passed the full suite, but the old one needed 64,305 lines of engine code and the new one did it in 9,908.
sentences.zh: 在《神鬼寓言 5》的组合中，新旧蜂群最终都通过了全套，但旧蜂群需要 64,305 行引擎代码，而新蜂群则需要 9,908 行。

sentences.en: The Opus mix shows the same shape with 19,013 lines at a 97% grade under the old harness, and 4,645 lines at 100% under the new harness.
sentences.zh: Opus 混合显示相同的形状，旧线束下有 19,013 条线，坡度为 97%，新线束下有 4,645 条线，坡度为 100%。

blocks.html_en: Model economics

sentences.en: Model economics
sentences.zh: 模型经济学

blocks.html_en: We said at the top that every model mix produced similar quality while the costs varied enormously, from $1,339 for the Opus 4.8 hybrid to $10,565 for GPT-5.5 alone. The token data shows where that difference comes from.

sentences.en: We said at the top that every model mix produced similar quality while the costs varied enormously, from $1,339 for the Opus 4.8 hybrid to $10,565 for GPT-5.5 alone.
sentences.zh: 我们在顶部说过，每种型号的组合都具有相似的质量，但成本差异很大，从 Opus 4.8 混合动力车的 1,339 美元到单独的 GPT-5.5 的 10,565 美元。

sentences.en: The token data shows where that difference comes from.
sentences.zh: 代币数据显示了这种差异的来源。

blocks.html_en: The structure of the spend was consistent across every run, with workers carrying at least 69% of the tokens, and over 90% in most.

sentences.en: The structure of the spend was consistent across every run, with workers carrying at least 69% of the tokens, and over 90% in most.
sentences.zh: 每次运行的支出结构都是一致的，工人携带至少 69% 的代币，大多数情况下超过 90%。

blocks.html_en: But the dollars split differently than the tokens, because planner tokens cost more. In the Opus 4.8 and Composer 2.5 mix, the Opus-as-planner produced a small fraction of the tokens but roughly two-thirds of the cost, while Composer-as-worker handled the vast majority of the tokens for the remaining third of the cost.

sentences.en: But the dollars split differently than the tokens, because planner tokens cost more.
sentences.zh: 但美元的分配方式与代币不同，因为规划者代币的成本更高。

sentences.en: In the Opus 4.8 and Composer 2.5 mix, the Opus-as-planner produced a small fraction of the tokens but roughly two-thirds of the cost, while Composer-as-worker handled the vast majority of the tokens for the remaining third of the cost.
sentences.zh: 在 Opus 4.8 和 Composer 2.5 的组合中，Opus-as-planner 生成了一小部分代币，但约占成本的三分之二，而 Composer-as-worker 则处理了绝大多数代币，并支付了剩余三分之一的成本。

blocks.html_en: Few moments in a large task genuinely require frontier intelligence, such as the original decomposition, the design decisions, and certain trade-offs. Once a frontier planner has collapsed the ambiguity into a detailed, explicit instruction, less expensive models simply have to follow it. This is a huge potential source of cost savings. In the run that used GPT-5.5 for both planners and workers, the workers alone cost $9,373. In the run where Opus 4.8 did the planning and Composer 2.5 did the work, the entire worker fleet cost $411.

sentences.en: Few moments in a large task genuinely require frontier intelligence, such as the original decomposition, the design decisions, and certain trade-offs.
sentences.zh: 大型任务中很少有时刻真正需要前沿智能，例如原始分解、设计决策和某些权衡。

sentences.en: Once a frontier planner has collapsed the ambiguity into a detailed, explicit instruction, less expensive models simply have to follow it.
sentences.zh: 一旦前沿规划者将模糊性分解为详细、明确的指令，较便宜的模型就必须遵循它。

sentences.en: This is a huge potential source of cost savings.
sentences.zh: 这是节省成本的巨大潜在来源。

sentences.en: In the run that used GPT-5.5 for both planners and workers, the workers alone cost $9,373.
sentences.zh: 在对计划者和工人都使用 GPT-5.5 的运行中，仅工人就花费了 9,373 美元。

sentences.en: In the run where Opus 4.8 did the planning and Composer 2.5 did the work, the entire worker fleet cost $411.
sentences.zh: 在 Opus 4.8 进行规划、Composer 2.5 进行工作的运行中，整个工作队列的成本为 411 美元。

blocks.html_en: One detail worth noting comes from comparing the two hybrid runs. The Fable 5 planner ran up a slightly smaller bill than the Opus 4.8 planner, despite roughly twice the per-token price, because it used far fewer planning tokens. But the Fable run's workers went through several times as many tokens, and the run as a whole came out substantially more expensive.

sentences.en: One detail worth noting comes from comparing the two hybrid runs.
sentences.zh: 一个值得注意的细节来自于对两次混合运行的比较。

sentences.en: The Fable 5 planner ran up a slightly smaller bill than the Opus 4.8 planner, despite roughly twice the per-token price, because it used far fewer planning tokens.
sentences.zh: 尽管每个代币的价格大约是 Opus 4.8 规划器的两倍，但 Fable 5 规划器的费用略低于 Opus 4.8 规划器，因为它使用的规划代币要少得多。

sentences.en: But the Fable run's workers went through several times as many tokens, and the run as a whole came out substantially more expensive.
sentences.zh: 但《神鬼寓言》运行的工作人员消耗了数倍的代币，整个运行的成本要高得多。

blocks.html_en: Specs as prompts

sentences.en: Specs as prompts
sentences.zh: 规格作为提示

blocks.html_en: Each jump in AI capability has raised the level of abstraction at which an engineer can work.

sentences.en: Each jump in AI capability has raised the level of abstraction at which an engineer can work.
sentences.zh: 人工智能能力的每一次飞跃都提高了工程师工作的抽象水平。

blocks.html_en: Autocomplete let engineers work one line of code at a time. Early models raised that to a block of code, and agents raised it to a file or a feature.

sentences.en: Autocomplete let engineers work one line of code at a time.
sentences.zh: 自动完成功能让工程师一次只处理一行代码。

sentences.en: Early models raised that to a block of code, and agents raised it to a file or a feature.
sentences.zh: 早期模型将其提升为代码块，代理将其提升为文件或功能。

blocks.html_en: With swarms, the unit of work becomes the spec.

sentences.en: With swarms, the unit of work becomes the spec.
sentences.zh: 对于群体，工作单元成为规范。

blocks.html_en: For that to work, the swarm has to actually follow the spec, which is what much of this post is about. We gave the swarm 835 pages of prose and it came back with a database. What was scarce in this experiment, and what we expect to be scarce in software engineering going forward, is the right description of intent.

sentences.en: For that to work, the swarm has to actually follow the spec, which is what much of this post is about.
sentences.zh: 为了实现这一点，群体必须真正遵循规范，这就是本文的大部分内容。

sentences.en: We gave the swarm 835 pages of prose and it came back with a database.
sentences.zh: 我们给了这群人 835 页的散文，他们带回来了一个数据库。

sentences.en: What was scarce in this experiment, and what we expect to be scarce in software engineering going forward, is the right description of intent.
sentences.zh: 这个实验中所缺乏的，以及我们预计未来软件工程中所缺乏的，是对意图的正确描述。

blocks.html_en: Seen this way, the swarm starts to resemble a compiler. A compiler translates source code down to machine code through a series of intermediate steps. The swarm does something similar with intent. Planners parse a goal into task trees, then lower it step by step into executable work. The difference is that a compiler preserves meaning at every step while the swarm is probabilistic at every one. Everything described in this post exists to close that gap.

sentences.en: Seen this way, the swarm starts to resemble a compiler.
sentences.zh: 从这个角度来看，群体开始变得像一个编译器。

sentences.en: A compiler translates source code down to machine code through a series of intermediate steps.
sentences.zh: 编译器通过一系列中间步骤将源代码翻译为机器代码。

sentences.en: The swarm does something similar with intent.
sentences.zh: 群体有意图地做类似的事情。

sentences.en: Planners parse a goal into task trees, then lower it step by step into executable work.
sentences.zh: 规划者将目标解析为任务树，然后逐步将其降低为可执行的工作。

sentences.en: The difference is that a compiler preserves meaning at every step while the swarm is probabilistic at every one.
sentences.zh: 不同之处在于，编译器在每一步都保留了意义，而群体在每一步都是概率性的。

sentences.en: Everything described in this post exists to close that gap.
sentences.zh: 这篇文章中描述的所有内容都是为了缩小这一差距。

blocks.html_en: We invite you to explore the swarm's output. The codebase from the solo Opus 4.8 run is public at github.com/cursor/minisqlite. Based on our initial glance it looks great, but we have not done a deeper manual analysis. Take your own look, and tell us what you find.

sentences.en: We invite you to explore the swarm's output.
sentences.zh: 我们邀请您探索群体的输出。

sentences.en: The codebase from the solo Opus 4.8 run is public at github.com/cursor/minisqlite.
sentences.zh: Opus 4.8 单独运行的代码库在 github.com/cursor/minisqlite 上公开。

sentences.en: Based on our initial glance it looks great, but we have not done a deeper manual analysis.
sentences.zh: 根据我们的初步观察，它看起来很棒，但我们还没有进行更深入的手动分析。

sentences.en: Take your own look, and tell us what you find.
sentences.zh: 亲自看看，然后告诉我们您发现了什么。

blocks.html_en: • To get a sense of solo frontier costs, we also ran Opus 4.8 and Fable 5 on their own. We graded those runs only informally, so we draw no conclusions about their quality here, though from experience we would expect both models to do well. Their costs are shown in the chart as the hatched bars. ↩

sentences.en: To get a sense of solo frontier costs, we also ran Opus 4.8 and Fable 5 on their own.
sentences.zh: 为了了解单独的前沿成本，我们还单独运行了 Opus 4.8 和 Fable 5。

sentences.en: We graded those runs only informally, so we draw no conclusions about their quality here, though from experience we would expect both models to do well.
sentences.zh: 我们只是非正式地对这些运行进行了评分，因此我们在这里不对它们的质量得出结论，尽管根据经验，我们预计这两个模型都会表现良好。

sentences.en: Their costs are shown in the chart as the hatched bars. ↩
sentences.zh: 它们的成本在图表中显示为阴影条。 ↩

blocks.html_en: To get a sense of solo frontier costs, we also ran Opus 4.8 and Fable 5 on their own. We graded those runs only informally, so we draw no conclusions about their quality here, though from experience we would expect both models to do well. Their costs are shown in the chart as the hatched bars. ↩

sentences.en: To get a sense of solo frontier costs, we also ran Opus 4.8 and Fable 5 on their own.

content_html_zh:
今年早些时候，我们进行了实验来测试缩放代理合作实现目标的极限。 我们的假设是，这将解锁新的任务规模和复杂性。

旗舰项目是一个长期运行的群体，从头开始构建一个网络浏览器。 它作为概念验证取得了成功，但距离完善的软件还差得很远。

这项工作故意是实证性的。 我们从一块空白的画布开始，一步步走向稳定、有效的系统。 从那时起，我们的目标就是充分了解代理群，以便有意识地对其进行设计。

为了测试这一进展，我们回到了旧群体一直在努力完成的一项任务：用 Rust 从头开始​​构建 SQLite，除了它的文档之外什么都没有。

我们的初步结果是有希望的。 我们使用相同的模型和相同的时间预算在同一任务上运行旧的和新的集群，并测量每个集群可以通过多少保留的 SQL 测试套件。

新群体在每个模型配置中都表现更好。 使用 Grok 4.5，它在四个小时内达到了 80%，而旧的集群则呈螺旋式上升，必须在第二个小时之前暂停。

我们还改变了哪些模型执行哪些工作。 在某些运行中，一个模型可以处理所有事情，而在其他运行中，则由前沿模型进行规划，而快速、廉价的模型则执行工作。 每种混合物的质量相似，但成本差异巨大。1

树木和树叶

对大型任务的描述自然采用树的形状，其根部的目标递归地细分为基本工作单元。 我们的群体有两个角色，都是围绕相同的树状分解组织的：

  • 规划代理在最智能的模型的支持下，将目标分解为多个部分并将其委托给他们。
  • 工作代理通常由更快、更便宜的模型驱动，执行这些部分。

该设计是更严格的编排系统的超集。 群体的形状不是对问题强加固定的拓扑，而是不断增长以覆盖问题的轮廓，并根据任务的复杂性按比例进行计算和上下文缩放。

我们认为这就是为什么该设计可以推广到构建浏览器、解决数学问题和优化 GPU 内核等多种任务。 我们还在内部使用它来查找和修复开源软件中的漏洞，提高我们自己的代码库的测试覆盖率，并生成数十亿个合成训练数据。

树对于记忆有什么作用

当单个智能体承担完整的任务时，它必须自己走遍整棵树，下降到每片叶子，同时始终保持其祖先、当前位置和更广泛的目标。

我们认为这解释了为什么长期运行的单一代理会发生漂移。 他们要么专注于眼前的工作，而忽视了大局，要么只顾大局，却把工作做得更差。

在群体中，计划者从不实施，因此其上下文永远不会充满低级细节，而工作人员从不计划，因此它可以将所有上下文都花在一项狭窄的工作上。

我们怀疑扩展代理群的能力来自于这种上下文效率，而不是来自并行性本身。 这种效率存在于各个规模的群体中，这就是为什么这种分解即使在中等规模的任务上也有助于代理的性能。

这种结构在其他地方也有类似的体现。 经济学家罗纳德·科斯（Ronald Coase）在询问企业存在的原因时指出，协调成本的增长速度比工作本身的增长速度还要快，因此组织会进入有界单位的层级，而不是让每个人都与每个人交谈。

代理的版本控制系统

在之前关于 swarm 的文章中，我们注意到像 Git 和 Cargo 这样的工具依赖于粗略锁来进行并发控制。 这对于一个开发人员来说很好，但对于数百个并发代理产生的工作量来说是不可行的。

今年早些时候，浏览器群在 Git 上的提交量达到了每小时约 1,000 次的峰值。 新系统的峰值速度约为每秒 1,000 次提交。

为了促进这种活动速度，我们从头开始构建了一个新的版本控制系统（VCS）。 吞吐量并不是拥有该层的唯一原因。 系统中的每个更改都会通过 VCS，因此它是冲突首先变得可见的地方，并且下一节中的几个协调机制直接在其内部实现。

每秒 1,000 次提交的故障模式

人类工程团队拥有标准的协调机制，例如代码审查、所有权、站立和合并队列。 这些系统按照人类的节奏工作，但按照群体的提交率，我们会看到人类团队通常不会遇到的故障模式。

裂脑设计

两个彼此不知情的规划者在代码库的不同部分以不同的方式实现相同的概念。

我们通过提示解决了这个问题。 规划者自己做出设计决策，而不是委托他们，我们要求他们确保没有两个委托子树决定相同的问题。

规划者之间的争论

一种更困难的争用形式是，两个计划者彼此了解，并在同一文件的来回更改中发生争执。

问题是现实的两张图片，合并工具无法解决分歧。 相反，我们让代理在共享设计文档中记录决策。 取决于决策的代码将经过编译检查的引用返回到其文档。 当规划者在不知不觉中相互矛盾时，协调器会合并文档，并且引用会将解决方案传播到下游。

合并冲突

在群体中，代理不断地在相同的文件上发生冲突。 为了解决碰撞，他们必须停下来，吸收其他智能体的上下文，并在其周围合并。 工作代理在这方面表现不佳，在实践中，要么覆盖其他更改，要么放弃自己的更改。

为了解决这个问题，我们创建了一个系统，其中中立的第三方代理干预合并冲突并代表所有各方解决它们。 它的唯一目标是公正和高效，类似于工程团队中合并队列的工作方式。

巨型文件

有些文件是特工特别受欢迎的工作场所。 每个代理可能只添加少量代码，并且没有任何一个代理负责保持文件较小。

这些“大文件”让一切都窒息。 它们的运输、区分和合并成本高昂，并且成为不断发生碰撞的场所。

为了解决这个问题，我们为工作代理提供了一种标记膨胀文件的方法。 一旦被标记，我们就会阻止新的提交，并且外部代理会将过度增长的文件分解为更小的模块。

骨化

代理通过在现有代码库中与人类参与循环的工作中了解到，即使需要更改，也不要触及核心代码。

为了解决这个问题，我们许可故意破坏。 判断核心更改值得的代理可以在其范围之外制作一个重点补丁，并留下评论解释为什么这样做。

编译器将更改传递到系统的其余部分，并且依赖于旧设计的所有内容都无法构建。 每个遇到这些错误之一的代理都会找到评论，阅读推理，并更新自己的工作以匹配。

回顾镜头

在一个长期运行的多智能体系统中，错误会累积，群体需要一种方法来纠正自己，以免小错误成为基础。

我们尝试了多种审​​查镜头，例如向审查代理提供工作人员的完整成绩单，或者仅提供其输出，或者只提供代码库。 我们还尝试了在不同模型上运行、接受不同培训和不同个性的审稿人。

没有单一的镜头可以捕捉到一切，但是解相关的镜头堆叠，自动驾驶系统无需任何单一完美组件即可达到高于人类的可靠性。 用于审核的计算量回报很高，因为审核比审核的工作便宜得多。 我们怀疑这种堆积的审查系统是维持运行质量的主要因素。

让代理人塑造环境

Stigmergy是蚂蚁和白蚁等群体生物在没有直接交流的情况下进行协调的机制。 它们塑造环境，环境塑造下一个有机体。

在早期的运行中，我们对“记笔记”和“记录决策”等规则进行了编码，因为它们看起来显然很好。 回想起来，他们让特工为未来的自己和队友将知识制度化。

我们在自己创作的共享环境中进行了一项实验，我们称之为“现场指南”，进一步推动了这一点。 这是一个完全由代理拥有的文件夹，其 index.md 在启动时会自动注入到每个代理中。 代理商的工作是策划指南中的内容，他们唯一的限制是直线预算。

该指南的基本逻辑是模型权重被冻结，因此正是值得捕获的意外遭遇，因此下一个智能体轨迹更短。

《现场指南》是一项早期实验，取得了可喜的成果。 我们预计代理不完全拥有的代码库的好处会更大。 训练模型为继任者写作，更好的捕捉会带来更好的回报，是一个有趣的后续研究领域。

SQLite 实验

我们指示新版本的 swarm 配备了上述所有改进，以 Rust 实现整个 835 页的 SQLite 手册。 我们保留了源代码、测试套件、SQLite 二进制文件和互联网访问权限。

为了衡量进度，我们根据 sqllogictest 进行评分，sqllogictest 是 SQLite 项目的一个测试套件，旨在检查不同的数据库引擎对于相同的查询是否返回相同的结果。 它包含数百万个已知正确答案的查询，等级是群体数据库正确的分数。 进度显示为跑步过程中的上升曲线。

蜂群从未被告知该套件的存在。 每次运行后，我们手动检查代码和运行本身，检查作弊和快捷方式，并确认系统是均匀构建的，而不仅仅是在测试看起来的地方。

当您阅读曲线时，请记住代理选择了自己的策略。 有些人打下了广泛的基础，在几个小时内得分很低，然后才出现后期峰值，而另一些人则深入某一领域，早期得分，然后在填补其余部分的同时保持稳定。 趋势比确切时刻的确切分数更重要。

模型组合的结果

我们测试了四种涵盖功能和成本的配置：

  1. GPT-5.5 既是规划者又是工作者。 贯穿始终的强大前沿模型2
  2. Grok 4.5 既是规划者又是工作者。 我们的具有成本效益的前沿模型作为比较点。
  3. Opus 4.8 作为计划者，Composer 2.5 作为工人。 前沿判断力与高效执行力相结合。
  4. 《神鬼寓言 5》作为策划者，《Composer 2.5》作为工人。 看看下一级规划者是否会让混合变得更有价值还是更不值得。

新安全带在各个方面的表现都优于旧安全带。

《神鬼寓言 5》混合动力车在第一个小时内就通过了大约三分之二的套房。 截至 4 小时截止时间，新运行的利用率在 73% 到 85% 之间，而旧运行的利用率在 11% 到 77% 之间。

旧的 Grok 4.5 运行在两小时之前暂停（更多内容见下文）。 每个新配置都 100% 通过了套件。

将来我们希望运行完整的 N×N 计划者-工作者组合矩阵。 对于这个周期，重要的是线束版本之间的比较，并且行为差异比分数差异显示的要大得多。

深入了解跑步

从最简单的活动衡量标准开始，我们可以看到 Grok 4.5 在旧工具和新工具下的提交率有何变化。 旧运行在前两个小时内产生了 68,000 次提交，大约是新运行速度的 70 倍。

一种解读是它的生产力更高。 另一个原因是，大多数提交都是忙碌的工作（冲击、争用、流失）。

合并冲突数据指向后一种解释。 在我们暂停之前，旧运行累积了超过 70,000 个冲突，加速而不是稳定，而新运行在整个四个小时内记录了不到 1000 个冲突。

冲突集中在文件增长最大的地方。 在旧的运行中，最大的文件在整个运行中不断增长，其单个最热门的文件收集了 7,771 个冲突，被 1,173 个不同的代理触及。 在新的运行中，整个代码库中争议最多的文件为 47 个。

旧群体最大的协调失败——脑裂，或者规划者互相重复工作——出现在包结构中。 Rust 代码被组织成称为 crate 的包，在这样的项目中，每个 crate 大致都是一个主要组件。

旧版本有 54 个包，其中包括三个独立的 SQL 包。 新的一批很早就确定了九个板条箱，并且再也没有添加过另一个。

所有这些都显示在最终的代码库中。 在《神鬼寓言 5》的组合中，新旧蜂群最终都通过了全套，但旧蜂群需要 64,305 行引擎代码，而新蜂群则需要 9,908 行。 Opus 混合显示相同的形状，旧线束下有 19,013 条线，坡度为 97%，新线束下有 4,645 条线，坡度为 100%。

模型经济学

我们在顶部说过，每种型号的组合都具有相似的质量，但成本差异很大，从 Opus 4.8 混合动力车的 1,339 美元到单独的 GPT-5.5 的 10,565 美元。 代币数据显示了这种差异的来源。

每次运行的支出结构都是一致的，工人携带至少 69% 的代币，大多数情况下超过 90%。

但美元的分配方式与代币不同，因为规划者代币的成本更高。 在 Opus 4.8 和 Composer 2.5 的组合中，Opus-as-planner 生成了一小部分代币，但约占成本的三分之二，而 Composer-as-worker 则处理了绝大多数代币，并支付了剩余三分之一的成本。

大型任务中很少有时刻真正需要前沿智能，例如原始分解、设计决策和某些权衡。 一旦前沿规划者将模糊性分解为详细、明确的指令，较便宜的模型就必须遵循它。 这是节省成本的巨大潜在来源。 在对计划者和工人都使用 GPT-5.5 的运行中，仅工人就花费了 9,373 美元。 在 Opus 4.8 进行规划、Composer 2.5 进行工作的运行中，整个工作队列的成本为 411 美元。

一个值得注意的细节来自于对两次混合运行的比较。 尽管每个代币的价格大约是 Opus 4.8 规划器的两倍，但 Fable 5 规划器的费用略低于 Opus 4.8 规划器，因为它使用的规划代币要少得多。 但《神鬼寓言》运行的工作人员消耗了数倍的代币，整个运行的成本要高得多。

规格作为提示

人工智能能力的每一次飞跃都提高了工程师工作的抽象水平。

自动完成功能让工程师一次只处理一行代码。 早期模型将其提升为代码块，代理将其提升为文件或功能。

对于群体，工作单元成为规范。

为了实现这一点，群体必须真正遵循规范，这就是本文的大部分内容。 我们给了这群人 835 页的散文，他们带回来了一个数据库。 这个实验中所缺乏的，以及我们预计未来软件工程中所缺乏的，是对意图的正确描述。

从这个角度来看，群体开始变得像一个编译器。 编译器通过一系列中间步骤将源代码翻译为机器代码。 群体有意图地做类似的事情。 规划者将目标解析为任务树，然后逐步将其降低为可执行的工作。 不同之处在于，编译器在每一步都保留了意义，而群体在每一步都是概率性的。 这篇文章中描述的所有内容都是为了缩小这一差距。

我们邀请您探索群体的输出。 Opus 4.8 单独运行的代码库在 github.com/cursor/minisqlite 上公开。 根据我们的初步观察，它看起来很棒，但我们还没有进行更深入的手动分析。 亲自看看，然后告诉我们您发现了什么。

  1. 为了了解单独的前沿成本，我们还单独运行了 Opus 4.8 和 Fable 5。 我们只是非正式地对这些运行进行了评分，因此我们在这里不对它们的质量得出结论，尽管根据经验，我们预计这两个模型都会表现良好。 它们的成本在图表中显示为阴影条。 ↩
  2. 我们希望 GPT-5.6 Sol 作为前沿配置。 与我们测试的其他模型相比，新模型似乎对字面意义和强调措辞更加敏感，而且我们遇到了与其他模型不同的失控螺旋。 没有时间调整最近发布的模型的提示，并且调整一个模型而保持其余模型不变会使比较不准确，因此我们退回到 GPT-5.5。 ↩
